mechanics.text:
INTRODUCTION
sents the setting and situation; through their characters the players interact with the story line and other characters_ Think of it this way: everyone has read book or seen movie in which the lead character does something that the read: er Or viewer finds so utterly wrong that he or she wants t0 shout out warning: But no matter what we say; the character will do what the plot   demands; we're just along the ride_ Even throwing popcorn won' nelp: roleplaying game_ the players control their characters' actions and respond to the events the plot: If the player does not want his Or her character t0 go through the door; the char acter will not: If the player thinks the character can talk him- or herself out  of tight situation rather than resorting to that trusty pistol, he 0r she can talk away: The script, or plot, of roleplaying game is flexible always changing based on the deci- sions the players make as characters The gamemaster controls the story: He or she keeps track of what is supposed happen when _ describes events as they occur SO that the players (as characters) can react t0 them_ keeps track other characters in the game (referred t0 as non- player characters , or NPCs), and resolves attempts t0 take action using the game system. The game system comes into play when char: acters seek t0 use their skills or otherwise do something that requires test to see whether they succeed, Specific rules are presented for situations that involve rolling dice to determine the outcome (see Game Concepts, p. 13). The gamemaster describes the world as the characters see functioning tneir eyes ears and other senses Gamemastering is not easy; but the thrill of creating an adven- ture that engages the other players' imaginations, testing their gaming skills and their characters skills in the game world, makes it worthwhile FASA publishes game supplements and adventures to help this process along; but good gamemasters always adapt the game universe to suit their own styles_ In roleplaying; stories (the adventures) evolve much like movie or book but within the flexible story line created by the gamemaster. The story iS the overall plot; general outline for what  might happen at certain times or in reaction to other events It is more concrete than that untii the  players become involved that point, the adventure becomes involving and dramatic as that great movie you saw last week Or that great book you stayed up all night to finish In some ways it'$ even better; because you helped create it.
Character Creation takes players through the process developing unique character tO portray in MechWarrior adven- tures By making series of choices about their characters lives before play begins players can actually create back" ground story for their characters at the same time they create game profile. Following this section is selection of Sample Characters with ready-made character   sheets that  beginning players can pick up and play immediately; or that the gamemas ter can use as ready-made non-player characters; The Traits and Skills sections detine the various special abilities characters can have in the game and provide complete game rules for using each of them The Combat section adds to the base rules from Game Concepts _ providing special rules for all kinds of combat situa tions, including the integration of the BattleTech game with your Mw3 characters. Following that is an Equipment guide that takes you through the process of acquiring gear for your characters special rules regarding equipment, and comprehensive cross section of the most useful and common weapons armor; elec tronics and other gear used in the thirty-first century: Though there wide variety BattleTech universe source material in separately available books Mw3 contains wealth of information so any player or gamemaster has all tney need to get started with only this book: The sourcebook section contains number essays various aspects the Battle Tech universe_ including Brief   History of  the Inner Sphere; Whistlestop Tour of the Inner Sphere; Government & Law; Society & Culture; The Clans and War and its Makers_ Along with these essays scrapbook describing the major affil- iations of the BattleTech universe provided in the color pages giving players complete overview of the vast tapestry of the universe_ Every gamemaster will find the Running the Game section useful, as it gives suggestions adventure and campaign frameworks selection of ready-made non-player characters and rules for awarding Experience Points t0 the player charac ters after their adventures At the end of the book iS selection of charts and tables that will be useful t0 character creation and game play; as well as blank character record sheet and char- acter creation worksheet.
THE MOST IMPORTANT RULE In spite of the large quantity of rules ad source material in this book; the most important rule of the game is simply to have fun. Gamemasters are allowed encouraged_ in fact ~to make alterations t0 the game rules ad setting to suit their play: ers tastes and whims_ Tne Battle Tech universe as it has devel- oped over the past fifteen years is remarkable place_ but if it doesn' match your style of play as-iS, go ahead and change itl This iS your game, after all: Enjoy it any way you like
HOW TO USE This BOOK This book is divided into number of sections, each with its own purpose in defining the MW3 game system and describ ing the BattleTech universe. The first section of the book, Game Concepts, should be read and understood by all players_ especially the gamemaster: It establishes the basis of the entire MW3 game system wiil be difficult to play without it.
